Title: Controlled decision experiment
Abstract
Participants completed a preregistered decision task. Results suggest a modest but reliable difference between treatment and control conditions.
Methods
Participants (N = 84) were randomly assigned to a treatment group or a control group. Materials, code, and raw data are openly available at https://osf.io/abcd1. The study was preregistered.
Results
The treatment group differed from the control group, p = 0.03, 95% CI [0.12, 0.78], Cohen's d = 0.42.
Discussion
These findings suggest a moderate effect consistent with the intervention hypothesis.
References
Smith, A. (2021). Example article. https://doi.org/10.1234/example.1
